title: The Garden Weasel

jokes.text: You folks see that flashing sign up there? Now, that sign says "applesauce." No, no, I'm kidding. It says "applause."
jokes.setup: Pointing to applause sign, claiming it says something else
jokes.punchline: Fake-out correction to the obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Simple warm-up comedian misdirection, functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Warm-up Host
dialogue.line: You folks see that flashing sign up there? Now, that sign says "applesauce." No, no, I'm kidding. It says "applause."

jokes.text: And now, because only he brings out the natural luster of the wood... Larry Sanders!
jokes.setup: Standard talk show introduction format
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical wood polish metaphor as intro reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character moment - overly earnest delivery of complete nonsense. Classic sidekick pomposity.

dialogue.line: And now, because only he brings out the natural luster of the wood... Larry Sanders!

jokes.text: You are already so much better than last night's audience... who came in here and looted the place. I wanna thank them for returning the couch and Hank.
jokes.setup: Complimenting tonight's audience over last night's
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd looting, then reducing Hank to furniture
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character dynamic - Larry's casual cruelty to Hank disguised as crowd work

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You are already so much better than last night's audience... who came in here and looted the place. I wanna thank them for returning the couch and Hank.

jokes.text: Well, good to know you're watching my competition.
jokes.setup: Audience cheers for Clinton on Arsenio
jokes.punchline: Dry acknowledgment of his own ratings problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character - Larry's insecurity showing through attempted confidence

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, good to know you're watching my competition.

jokes.text: Actually, I'm a little frustrated because we had Jerry Brown on this show. He didn't actually play a number with the band... but he did score them some great coke.
jokes.setup: Comparing Jerry Brown appearance to Clinton's musical performance
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'score' - musical vs. drug dealing
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with edge - typical early 90s political irreverence

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Actually, I'm a little frustrated because we had Jerry Brown on this show. He didn't actually play a number with the band... but he did score them some great coke.

jokes.text: I was really leaning towards voting... for the Captain of Captain and Tennille... because evidently, he at least has some military experience.
jokes.setup: Discussing California political candidates
jokes.punchline: Taking 'Captain' literally as actual military rank
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist logic, though feels more like standard stand-up material

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I was really leaning towards voting... for the Captain of Captain and Tennille... because evidently, he at least has some military experience.

jokes.text: Ted Kennedy got married this last weekend... and boy, he's gonna be in for a surprise when he sobers up, huh?
jokes.setup: Ted Kennedy wedding announcement
jokes.punchline: Implying he was too drunk to remember getting married
jokes.explanation: Predictable Kennedy drinking joke - relies on well-worn stereotype

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Ted Kennedy got married this last weekend... and boy, he's gonna be in for a surprise when he sobers up, huh?

jokes.text: - I liked it very much. - Well, you're mistaken.
jokes.setup: Hank approves of Larry's joke
jokes.punchline: Larry's flat dismissal of Hank's opinion
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Larry's casual cruelty to Hank with deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I liked it very much.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, you're mistaken.

jokes.text: I swear, I feel I would be doing one of those CNN interviews... looking into the camera going, "Come on, Suzie. You know you did it with me. Say something."
jokes.setup: Joke about no women coming forward about affairs if he ran for president
jokes.punchline: Pathetic pleading for validation on national TV
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Larry's insecurity and desperation for validation, perfectly cringe

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I swear, I feel I would be doing one of those CNN interviews... looking into the camera going, "Come on, Suzie. You know you did it with me. Say something."

jokes.text: His Carol Channing was terrific. It was. You know, Patriot Games should have been a musical.
jokes.setup: Discussing Harrison Ford's supposed impressions
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical suggestion about action movie being musical
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist escalation in the post-show banter

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: His Carol Channing was terrific.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It was.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You know, Patriot Games should have been a musical.

jokes.text: You could see her thigh between that short skirt and the boots? - I still see it.
jokes.setup: Hank getting inappropriate about Shirley Jones
jokes.punchline: Larry's disturbing admission of ongoing fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe character moment - Larry's inappropriate admission

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You could see her thigh between that short skirt and the boots?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I still see it.

jokes.text: - The monologue. - There's a monologue in every show.
jokes.setup: Paula claims to have watched and cites favorite part
jokes.punchline: Larry's flat observation about the generic nature of her answer
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Larry's paranoid suspicion delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: The monologue.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: There's a monologue in every show.

jokes.text: I'll come over later and stick a red hot poker up your ass. We'll call it even. - Okay. You have my address, right? - And your poker size.
jokes.setup: Artie threatens violent punishment for skipping meeting
jokes.punchline: Larry treating violent threat as logistics question
jokes.explanation: Excellent character dynamic - Larry's passive-aggressive response to Artie's aggression

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'll come over later and stick a red hot poker up your ass. We'll call it even.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Okay. You have my address, right?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And your poker size.

jokes.text: A network president shouldn't be allowed to say Hello, Larry.
jokes.setup: Sheldon greets Larry normally
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid interpretation of innocent greeting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's insecurity about power dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: A network president shouldn't be allowed to say Hello, Larry.

jokes.text: What do we do to keep our advertisers happy... other than giving them free hand jobs?
jokes.setup: Corporate speak about pleasing advertisers
jokes.punchline: Jumping to crude sexual service
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation showing the desperation of network television

dialogue.speaker: Sheldon
dialogue.line: What do we do to keep our advertisers happy... other than giving them free hand jobs?

jokes.text: Well, you know, knowing the sponsors the way I do... I just think they would respond more to the hand jobs.
jokes.setup: Network executive mentioned hand jobs as alternative to commercials
jokes.punchline: Larry earnestly advocating for the crude alternative
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Larry's characteristic inappropriate earnestness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, you know, knowing the sponsors the way I do... I just think they would respond more to the hand jobs.

jokes.text: I didn't know that. Hank's the fucking Green Giant?
jokes.setup: Learning Hank only appears from knees down in Green Giant ads
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked reaction to mundane commercial trivia
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Larry's misplaced priorities and shock

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I didn't know that. Hank's the fucking Green Giant?

jokes.text: Gee, I didn't know we were still owned by the garage-door opener people.
jokes.setup: Learning about parent company Unidac Electronics
jokes.punchline: Reducing corporate conglomerate to single mundane product
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire - the absurdity of entertainment corporate ownership

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Gee, I didn't know we were still owned by the garage-door opener people.

jokes.text: That is no way to talk about Miss Parish.
jokes.setup: Melanie says the commercial is for 'The Garden Weasel'
jokes.punchline: Larry pretending 'weasel' refers to her personally
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection joke with character conflict undertone

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That is no way to talk about Miss Parish.

jokes.text: Garden Weasel, honey. It's the Garden Weasel. - Even funnier. - The Garden Weasel. Weasel's funnier.
jokes.setup: Discussion of product name
jokes.punchline: Meta-analysis of which version is funnier
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy moment about comedy itself, mildly amusing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Garden Weasel, honey. It's the Garden Weasel.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The Garden Weasel.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Weasel's funnier.

jokes.text: I'm sure that bitch Melanie's laughing her ass off.
jokes.setup: Discussing the commercial situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid assumption about Melanie's malicious enjoyment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's paranoia and misogyny

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm sure that bitch Melanie's laughing her ass off.

jokes.text: Artie said when we got out of the meeting... 'I swear I killed that woman in Korea.'
jokes.setup: Recounting Artie's reaction to Melanie
jokes.punchline: Artie's casual reference to wartime killing
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Artie - casual reference to dark past

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Artie said when we got out of the meeting... 'I swear I killed that woman in Korea.'

jokes.text: Honey, it's not your Make Me Laugh audition tape, is it?
jokes.setup: Larry about to show a commercial from his past
jokes.punchline: Jeannie's fear of seeing embarrassing audition footage
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Larry's potentially embarrassing past

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Honey, it's not your Make Me Laugh audition tape, is it?

jokes.text: From the Pan Am files. I was halfway to Fort Worth, when all of a sudden it hit me... I'd left my briefcase back at the terminal at JFK.
jokes.setup: Watching Larry's old Pan Am commercial
jokes.punchline: The earnest, dated corporate testimonial style
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - seeing Larry's embarrassing commercial past

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: From the Pan Am files. I was halfway to Fort Worth, when all of a sudden it hit me... I'd left my briefcase back at the terminal at JFK.

jokes.text: I'm not in a position to say no. They'll think I'm an asshole. They'll think I'm not a team player.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he can't refuse the commercial
jokes.punchline: His paranoid spiral about career consequences
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Larry's insecurity and catastrophic thinking

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm not in a position to say no. They'll think I'm an asshole. They'll think I'm not a team player.

jokes.text: I'm a day away from just being the funny weather man in Tuscon. 'Oh, but on this good side, it's dry heat.'
jokes.setup: Larry's career fears escalating
jokes.punchline: Specific vision of career rock bottom with weak weather joke
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - specific fear with bad joke example showing his comedy anxiety

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm a day away from just being the funny weather man in Tuscon. 'Oh, but on this good side, it's dry heat.'

jokes.text: Paula says Robert Hays is booked on the show tonight... but he fell in the shower and he's seeing shapes and colors and stuff.
jokes.setup: Guest availability update
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery of serious head injury symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist setup for guest situation

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Paula says Robert Hays is booked on the show tonight... but he fell in the shower and he's seeing shapes and colors and stuff.

jokes.text: Perfect. I won't ask him anything tough.
jokes.setup: Learning guest has possible concussion
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical accommodation for brain injury
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan reaction showing Larry's pragmatic selfishness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Perfect. I won't ask him anything tough.

jokes.text: Permission to speak freely? - Permission granted.
jokes.setup: Hank requesting formal permission to speak
jokes.punchline: Larry playing along with military formality
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Hank's formality and Larry's patronizing indulgence

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Permission to speak freely?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Permission granted.

jokes.text: That one Green Giant spot has really been a monkey on my back.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining his commercial overexposure
jokes.punchline: Treating invisible background work as career burden
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character moment - overblowing minor career detail

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That one Green Giant spot has really been a monkey on my back.

jokes.text: Mommy? Mommy, why can't I ride the dog?
jokes.setup: Robert pretending to be concussed
jokes.punchline: Childlike regression question
jokes.explanation: Guest's attempt at physical comedy, somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Robert Hays
dialogue.line: Mommy? Mommy, why can't I ride the dog?

jokes.text: Mommy? Mommy, why can't I ride the dog? I had you going, didn't I? I had you going.
jokes.setup: Robert Hays appearing to have serious head injury symptoms
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's an act from his comedy background
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on audience expectations about the head injury setup

dialogue.speaker: Robert Hays
dialogue.line: Mommy? Mommy, why can't I ride the dog? I had you going, didn't I? I had you going.

jokes.text: I swear I killed her in the war. - You used that line yesterday.
jokes.setup: Artie repeats his Korea killing line
jokes.punchline: Larry pointing out it's become a repetitive bit
jokes.explanation: Good meta-callback joke about character's repetitive nature

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I swear I killed her in the war.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You used that line yesterday.

jokes.text: It's just that I don't even own a Garden Weasel. Never used one. It's a little unethical, don't you think?
jokes.setup: Larry's moral concerns about the commercial
jokes.punchline: Finding ethics in the wrong place given his industry
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Larry's misplaced moral boundaries

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It's just that I don't even own a Garden Weasel. Never used one. It's a little unethical, don't you think?

jokes.text: Unethical? Geez, Larry, don't start pulling at that thread. Our whole world will unravel.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about commercial ethics
jokes.punchline: Artie's acknowledgment of industry-wide dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Excellent industry satire - perfectly crafted line about entertainment dishonesty

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Unethical? Geez, Larry, don't start pulling at that thread. Our whole world will unravel.

jokes.text: Yeah, you know not what you applaud for.
jokes.setup: Audience applauds Robert's nakedness description
jokes.punchline: Larry's biblical-style warning about their enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with biblical reference

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, you know not what you applaud for.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you caught the reflection in that magnifying makeup mirror.
jokes.setup: Robert Hays saying he was shocked by his reflection in the mirror
jokes.punchline: Larry suggesting it was magnified, implying Hays was shocked by his small anatomy
jokes.explanation: Decent setup/punchline playing on male insecurity, though somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, maybe you caught the reflection in that magnifying makeup mirror.

jokes.text: I'm about 100 yards off the green here. I'll try a five Weasel.
jokes.setup: Larry doing the Garden Weasel commercial
jokes.punchline: Treating garden tool like golf club
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist commercial subversion

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm about 100 yards off the green here. I'll try a five Weasel.

jokes.text: And by the way, it's been years since I've said that.
jokes.setup: Larry says 'glide your Weasel'
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the sexual innuendo awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo acknowledgment, somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And by the way, it's been years since I've said that.

jokes.text: And by the way, it's been years since I've said that.
jokes.setup: Larry saying 'glide your Weasel'
jokes.punchline: Meta comment acknowledging the sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment with Larry breaking character to acknowledge innuendo

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And by the way, it's been years since I've said that.

jokes.text: I hit Jimmy Hoffa.
jokes.setup: Larry pretending to garden
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have dug up famous missing person
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy moment with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I hit Jimmy Hoffa.

jokes.text: I feel quite certain our client didn't expect to hear... the name of a dead Teamster used as a selling point.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Larry's commercial performance
jokes.punchline: Formal corporate speak about Hoffa reference
jokes.explanation: Good corporate-speak reaction to Larry's transgression

dialogue.speaker: Melanie
dialogue.line: I feel quite certain our client didn't expect to hear... the name of a dead Teamster used as a selling point.

jokes.text: You know the truly frightening thing? - What? - I'm really turned on right now.
jokes.setup: Larry reflecting on confrontation with Melanie
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual response to conflict
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's inappropriate psychology

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You know the truly frightening thing?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm really turned on right now.

jokes.text: I understand that. I used to be single.
jokes.setup: Dog trainer explaining behavior modification through positive reinforcement and treats
jokes.punchline: Larry equating it to his past dating experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor connecting dog training to dating with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I understand that. I used to be single.

jokes.text: I understand that. I used to be single.

jokes.punchline: Larry connecting dog training to dating

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I understand that. I used to be single.

jokes.text: You tell that Korean woman that she's wrong. - She is not Korean. Everybody thinks she's Korean.
jokes.setup: Larry calling Melanie Korean again
jokes.punchline: Jeannie's exasperated correction about misconception
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about Larry's assumptions

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You tell that Korean woman that she's wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: She is not Korean. Everybody thinks she's Korean.

jokes.explanation: Good absurdist alternative with quotable quality

dialogue.line: Why not just call it the Amazing Rat Stick?

jokes.setup: Following up Amazing Rat Stick

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Should I have said the Fabulous Vermin Instrument?

jokes.text: If you were a guy, I'd take you outside right now. - If you were a guy, I'd go.
jokes.setup: Larry threatening to fight if Melanie were male
jokes.punchline: Melanie's confident response suggesting she'd win
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Melanie's toughness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: If you were a guy, I'd take you outside right now.

dialogue.speaker: Melanie
dialogue.line: If you were a guy, I'd go.

jokes.text: I was tying my shoes this morning... I walked into the door, bumped my head. - Whatever. - No, that's what really happened. - All right, a woman hit me.

jokes.punchline: Finally admitting the truth after weak denial
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with character embarrassment

jokes.text: America. Everything she stands for.
jokes.setup: Larry asks what Hank thinks about during pearl cream commercials
jokes.punchline: Hank's earnest patriotic response to beauty product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - earnest patriotism applied to cosmetics commercial. Classic character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: America. Everything she stands for.

jokes.text: Hello, Garden Weasel!
jokes.setup: Hank asks audience to say hello to the product
jokes.punchline: Audience enthusiastically greeting gardening tool
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment of audience participation with garden tool

dialogue.speaker: Audience
dialogue.line: Hello, Garden Weasel!

jokes.text: This Garden Weasel does the work of ten men and a boy. - How many boys, Hank? - One small boy.
jokes.setup: Hank's exaggerated product claim
jokes.punchline: Larry's literal clarification of the absurd math
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic with Larry's straight-man clarification

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: This Garden Weasel does the work of ten men and a boy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: How many boys, Hank?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: One small boy.

jokes.text: And if Unidac Electronics has a little problem with this... they can screw a lightbulb up my ass and use me as a desk lamp.
jokes.setup: Larry refusing to do more commercials
jokes.punchline: Grotesque visual metaphor for corporate exploitation
jokes.explanation: Excellent grotesque metaphor for corporate control - memorable and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And if Unidac Electronics has a little problem with this... they can screw a lightbulb up my ass and use me as a desk lamp.

jokes.text: Don't take this as a threat, but I killed a man like you in Korea... hand to hand.
jokes.setup: Artie protecting Larry from Melanie
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to wartime killing with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Great character callback with added menacing detail - Artie's protective loyalty

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't take this as a threat, but I killed a man like you in Korea... hand to hand.

jokes.text: Don't take this as a threat, but I killed a man like you in Korea... hand to hand.
jokes.setup: Artie confronting Melanie after Larry's blowup
jokes.punchline: Artie's threat while claiming it's not a threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie callback with the absurd disclaimer that makes the threat even more menacing

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't take this as a threat, but I killed a man like you in Korea... hand to hand.

tmdb_overview: Over Larry's strong protests, network executives force him to do a live commercial on the show for one of his sponsors.
